<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I have a clear picture of Phase 9 and am ready to summarize its planned implementation.

Phase 9 will add explicit workflows for edge cases. Key items include missing birth certificates, unrecognized marriages, duplicate identity flags, illegal stays, refugee paths, diplomat rules, homeless addresses, and EU versus non-EU policies. Each item needs domain rules, UI branches, and dedicated tests. Some scaffolding from Phase 7 is already in place.

Phase 9 still needs automatic exception triggers, full duplicate investigation workflows, and referral flows for illegal stays. The BirthCertificate document type and duplicate detection warnings are already in place.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

## Phase 9 — Exception scenarios

In [docs/ROADMAP.md](docs/ROADMAP.md), Phase 9 maps to **IDEA.md’s “Major Exceptions”** section. The goal is not new happy-path intake, but **explicit workflows for edge cases** — each slice adds domain rules, a UI branch, and tests.

| Slice | Priority | What it would mean in practice |
|-------|----------|--------------------------------|
| **Missing birth certificate → suspend** | High | Detect missing/unacceptable birth cert (or parents info), block approval, guide officer to suspend with a specific reason and follow-up checklist (translation, legalisation, replacement, court decision). |
| **Duplicate identity investigation** | High | Turn today’s NR “possible duplicate” warning into a real workflow: investigation flag, block registration until resolved, link-or-reject path, audit trail. |
| **EU vs non-EU policy differences (deepen)** | High | Extend beyond today’s 3 categories (`EuCitizen`, `NonEuWorker`, `Student`) and 3 policies — richer document/permit rules, annex types, category-specific checklist blockers. |
| **Marriage not recognised** | Medium | Workflow when civil status is “married abroad” but recognition is pending — suspend, keep person as single for now, track recognition procedure. |
| **Illegal stay → reject + refer** | Medium | When residence basis is absent/invalid, structured reject + referral notes (immigration office / federal path), not just a dropdown reason. |
| **Refugee / temporary protection** | Medium | New residence categories, federal-decision dependencies, different register targets and document sets. |
| **Diplomat separate rules** | Low | Separate category, lighter/different permit checks, possibly different register target. |
| **Homeless reference address** | Low | Reference-address or designated-mechanism intake path instead of a normal domicile. |

Each slice follows the standard checklist: domain method(s), handler, endpoint if needed, migration, Blazor UI, integration test, seed/glossary as needed.

---

## What you already have (scaffolding, not full workflows)

Phase 7 laid groundwork that Phase 9 builds on:

- **Reject / suspend / resume** with reasons like `DuplicateIdentity`, `IllegalStay`, `MarriageRecognitionPending`
- **NR duplicate hint** on case detail (warning + search/link dialog)
- **`BirthCertificate`** document type and attachment model
- **Basic immigration policies** for EU, non-EU worker, student only

Phase 9 turns those **labels and warnings** into **guided, rule-driven branches** — e.g. “missing birth cert” should proactively affect the checklist and suggest suspend, not rely on the officer picking a reason manually.

---

## Do I need more input?

**Yes, a little — mainly for ordering and depth**, not for a high-level go/no-go.

Useful decisions before coding:

1. **Which slice first?** Roadmap suggests high-priority trio: missing birth cert, duplicate investigation, EU/non-EU deepen. Confirm that order or pick one vertical demo (e.g. “duplicate investigation end-to-end”).
2. **Fidelity level** — demo-quality rules (one clear path per exception) vs. closer to Belgian admin reality (multiple sub-cases per exception). Refugee/diplomat/homeless are especially sensitive here.
3. **Scope of “EU vs non-EU deepen”** — e.g. add categories (`Refugee`, `FamilyReunification`, …) in this phase, or only tighten rules on existing three?
4. **Referral / investigation artefacts** — is a notes + audit + status flag enough, or do you want persisted entities (e.g. `InvestigationCase`, `ReferralRecord`)?

I don’t need a full municipal legal spec to start, but **one chosen first slice + depth target** avoids rework.

---

## Plan mode?

**Yes — Plan mode is a good idea before implementation.**

Phase 9 is a good fit because:

- It’s **many independent slices** with different domain trade-offs
- There’s **no detailed phase doc yet** (unlike Phase 7 / 8.1)
- Several items **overlap** (refugee path vs EU/non-EU deepen vs new categories)
- You’ll want to agree **sprint packaging** and **what “done” looks like** per slice

A short planning pass could produce: slice order, domain sketch per slice, what stays enum-only vs new entities, and a Phase 9 doc in `docs/phases/` before any code.

If you want to stay in Agent mode, the lowest-risk start is **Slice 1: Missing birth certificate → suspend** — narrow, high priority, and mostly extends existing suspend/checklist/document logic.

Which do you prefer: **Plan mode to design Phase 9**, or **Agent mode starting with one slice** (and which one)?

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Ready to formulate a comprehensive plan for phase 9.

Phase 9 covers birth evidence tracking and duplicate investigation blocking.

Birth evidence requires a certificate or waiver with suspension. The checklist flags established status. The step UI and review logic update accordingly.

Duplicate cases block approval until resolved. The system auto-suggests suspension and may suggest rejection.

Identity document requirements vary by policy category. EU policies check passports and national ID cards. Non-EU policies may check identity documents too. Re-evaluation triggers on document attach or remove.

Marriage recognition tracks status as Recognised, Pending, or NotApplicable. Pending foreign marriages record Single status for registration and suspend the pending flag. The UI lives in the CivilStatusStep.

Residence policy failures without valid permits or immigration decisions mark the case as an illegal stay candidate.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Creating the Phase 9 plan for the six selected slices (high + medium priority).

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>

Parameters:

````json
{
  "name": "Phase 9 Exceptions",
  "overview": "Implement Phase 9 high + medium exception slices: missing birth certificate, duplicate investigation, EU/non-EU policy deepening, marriage recognition, illegal stay referral, and refugee/temporary protection — turning existing reject/suspend enums into rule-driven workflows with checklist gates, UI guidance, and tests. Diplomat and homeless slices are deferred.",
  "plan": "# Phase 9 — Exception Scenarios (High + Medium)\n\n**Scope:** 6 of 8 roadmap slices. **Deferred:** diplomat rules, homeless reference address (low priority).\n\n**Goal:** Move from manual officer enum selection to **domain-driven exception detection** — checklist blockers, guided UI alerts, and suggested suspend/reject paths — reusing the Phase 7 decision state machine ([`RegistrationCase.Reject/Suspend`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs)).\n\n---\n\n## Current foundation (reuse, don't rebuild)\n\n| Already exists | Phase 9 extends it |\n|----------------|-------------------|\n| `RejectionReason` / `SuspensionReason` enums + dialogs | Auto-suggest reasons from domain rules |\n| `RegistrationCaseChecklist` (5 flags) | Add exception flags; extend `IsReadyForApproval` |\n| `ResidencePolicyEvaluator` + 3 policies | Deepen EU policy; add `RefugeePolicy` |\n| NR duplicate scoring + link flow | Investigation gate blocking approval |\n| `DocumentType.BirthCertificate` + upload | Requirement enforcement |\n| `RegisterTarget.WaitingRegister` (unused) | Wire for refugee path |\n| `ImmigrationDecisionReference` on case | Required for refugee policy |\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph triggers [Re-evaluation triggers]\n        AttachDoc[AttachDocumentHandler]\n        RemoveDoc[RemoveDocumentHandler]\n        RecordIdentity[RecordIdentityHandler]\n        RecordBirth[RecordBirthInformationHandler]\n        CivilStatus[RecordCivilStatusHandler]\n        SetCategory[SetResidenceCategoryHandler]\n        RecordDecision[RecordImmigrationDecisionHandler]\n    end\n\n    subgraph eval [RegistrationExceptionEvaluator]\n        BirthRule[BirthCertificateRule]\n        DupRule[DuplicateInvestigationRule]\n        ResidenceRule[ResidencePolicyEvaluator]\n        MarriageRule[MarriageRecognitionRule]\n    end\n\n    subgraph aggregate [RegistrationCase]\n        Checklist[RegistrationCaseChecklist]\n        DupStatus[DuplicateInvestigationStatus]\n        ApproveGate[IsReadyForApproval]\n    end\n\n    triggers --\u003e eval\n    eval --\u003e aggregate\n    ApproveGate --\u003e CaseDecisionSection\n```\n\n**New cross-cutting service:** [`RegistrationExceptionEvaluator`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/RegistrationExceptionEvaluator.cs) — called from handlers after mutations (mirrors [`RegistrationResidenceEvaluator`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/RegistrationResidenceEvaluator.cs)). Keeps handlers thin; all rules testable in domain.\n\n**Critical fix:** [`AttachDocumentHandler`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/AttachDocument/AttachDocumentHandler.cs) currently does **not** re-evaluate policies — must call exception evaluator + residence evaluator after upload/remove.\n\n---\n\n## Delivery order (6 slices)\n\nImplement in this order because later slices depend on earlier infrastructure.\n\n### Slice 0 — Foundation (prerequisite for all)\n\n**Domain**\n- Extend [`RegistrationCaseChecklist`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCaseChecklist.cs):\n  - `BirthEvidenceEstablished`\n  - `DuplicateInvestigationResolved`\n- Add `DuplicateInvestigationStatus` enum on `RegistrationCase`: `None | Open | ResolvedLinked | ResolvedDistinct`\n- Update `IsReadyForApproval` to require new flags\n- EF migration for new owned columns\n\n**Web**\n- `RegistrationExceptionEvaluator` with pluggable rules (internal static helpers or small rule classes in Domain)\n- Wire into: `AttachDocument`, `RemoveDocument`, `RecordIdentity`, `LinkExistingPerson`, `GetRegistrationCaseHandler` (duplicate refresh)\n- Extend [`CaseReviewChecklistMapper`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/GetCaseReviewChecklist/CaseReviewChecklistMapper.cs) with 2 new questions (birth evidence, duplicate investigation)\n- Update [`RegistrationCaseDetailDto`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/GetRegistrationCase/RegistrationCaseDetailDto.cs) + checklist DTO\n\n---\n\n### Slice 9.1 — Missing birth certificate → suspend (High)\n\n**Domain**\n- Add `BirthInformation` value object on `Person`: `BirthPlace` (required), optional `BirthCountry`\n- `RecordBirthInformation()` on `RegistrationCase` → sets person birth info, calls birth rule\n- **Birth rule:** `BirthEvidenceEstablished = true` when birth place recorded **and** case has attached `BirthCertificate` document\n\n**Web slice:** `RecordBirthInformation` (handler, validator, endpoint)\n\n**UI**\n- New [`BirthInformationStep.razor`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/BirthInformationStep.razor) — follow [`CivilStatusStep.razor`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/CivilStatusStep.razor) pattern\n- Add `BirthInformation` to [`RegistrationIntakeStep`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/RegistrationIntakeStep.cs) (after CivilStatus, before PoliceVerification)\n- Insert collapsible section in [`RegistrationCaseDetail.razor`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseDetail.razor)\n- **`AppAlert` Warning** when birth place recorded but no birth certificate: *\"Registration may be delayed — suspend until certificate (translation/legalisation) is on file.\"*\n- Link to document panel; pre-select `SuspensionReason.MissingDocuments` in suspend dialog when opened from alert\n\n**Tests:** domain birth rule; integration `RecordBirthInformation` + attach cert clears checklist; suspend/resume with `MissingDocuments` preserves existing [`RegistrationCaseDecisionTests`](tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/Registration/RegistrationCaseDecisionTests.cs) pattern\n\n---\n\n### Slice 9.2 — EU vs non-EU policy deepen (High)\n\n**Domain**\n- [`EuCitizenPolicy`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Immigration/Policies/EuCitizenPolicy.cs): require `Passport` **or** `NationalIdCard` in `AttachedDocumentTypes` (context already passed, currently ignored)\n- [`NonEuWorkerPolicy`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Immigration/Policies/NonEuWorkerPolicy.cs) / [`StudentPolicy`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Immigration/Policies/StudentPolicy.cs): add identity document check alongside permit check\n- Return descriptive `ResidencePolicyResult.Invalid(...)` messages for missing docs vs expired permit\n\n**Web**\n- Re-evaluate residence on attach/remove document (via exception evaluator calling `RegistrationResidenceEvaluator`)\n- [`ResidenceStep.razor`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/ResidenceStep.razor): show document-specific policy messages (EU needs passport/ID; non-EU needs permit + identity doc)\n\n**Tests:** extend [`ResidencePolicyTests`](tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/) — EU without passport fails; EU with passport passes; attach document integration clears `LegalResidenceEstablished`\n\n---\n\n### Slice 9.3 — Duplicate identity investigation (High)\n\n**Domain**\n- On identity recorded (or case loaded with unscored duplicates): if NR match score ≥ 80 and person not linked → set `DuplicateInvestigationStatus = Open`, `DuplicateInvestigationResolved = false`\n- On `LinkExistingPerson` → `ResolvedLinked`, resolved = true\n- New domain method `ResolveDuplicateAsDistinct(officer, notes)` → `ResolvedDistinct` (officer confirms different person after investigation)\n- **Block `Approve()`** while status is `Open`\n\n**Web slice:** `ResolveDuplicateInvestigation` (handler + endpoint)\n\n**UI**\n- Enhance existing duplicate warning in [`RegistrationCaseDetail.razor`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseDetail.razor):\n  - Open investigation: prominent warning + actions *Link existing* / *Confirm distinct person*\n  - Resolved: info chip\n- [`CaseDecisionSection`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/CaseDecisionSection.razor): disable Approve with blocking reason; suggest `SuspensionReason.InvestigationRequired` or `RejectionReason.DuplicateIdentity`\n\n**Tests:** scorer threshold triggers open status; approve blocked; link resolves; distinct resolution allows approval; integration in [`NationalRegisterTests`](tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/NationalRegisterTests.cs)\n\n---\n\n### Slice 9.4 — Refugee / temporary protection (Medium)\n\n**Domain**\n- Add `ResidenceCategory.Refugee` to [`ResidenceCategory.cs`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Immigration/ResidenceCategory.cs)\n- New `RefugeePolicy`: valid only when `ImmigrationDecisionReference` present on case; otherwise invalid with message *\"Awaiting federal protection decision\"*\n- [`RegisterTargetResolver`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegisterTargetResolver.cs): `Refugee` → `WaitingRegister` when decision present, null suggestion when missing\n- Auto-flag: missing decision → checklist blocks register determinability; UI suggests `SuspensionReason.AwaitingFederalDecision`\n\n**Web**\n- Register policy in [`DependencyInjection.cs`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/DependencyInjection.cs)\n- [`ResidenceStep.razor`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/ResidenceStep.razor): add Refugee category option; show immigration decision sub-form (reuse existing `RecordImmigrationDecision` handler)\n\n**Tests:** refugee without decision → not ready; with decision → `WaitingRegister` suggested; approve with waiting register target\n\n---\n\n### Slice 9.5 — Illegal stay → reject + refer (Medium)\n\n**Domain**\n- Extend residence evaluation: when category set, no valid permit, **no** immigration decision, and policy returns invalid → expose `IllegalStayDetected` computed flag on case (not persisted — derived from policy state)\n- Block approval; officer must reject\n\n**Web**\n- [`CaseDecisionSection`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/CaseDecisionSection.razor): when `IllegalStayDetected`, show **`AppAlert` Error** with referral guidance: *\"Population cannot regularise immigration status — reject and refer to Immigration Office.\"*\n- Pre-fill reject dialog with `RejectionReason.IllegalStay` + template notes\n\n**Tests:** non-EU worker, no permit, no decision → illegal stay flag; reject path integration; audit trail\n\n---\n\n### Slice 9.6 — Marriage not recognised (Medium)\n\n**Domain**\n- Add `MarriageRecognitionStatus` on [`CivilStatusRecord`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Identity/CivilStatusRecord.cs): `NotApplicable | Recognised | PendingRecognition`\n- When status is `Married`/`RegisteredPartnership` and `MarriagePlace` is outside Belgium (simple string check: not containing \"Belgium\"/\"België\") → officer must set recognition status\n- **Rule:** `PendingRecognition` → effective register civil status treated as `Single` for display; suggest `SuspensionReason.MarriageRecognitionPending`; block approval until `Recognised`\n\n**Web**\n- Extend [`RecordCivilStatus`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/RecordCivilStatus/) request with `MarriageRecognitionStatus`\n- [`CivilStatusStep.razor`](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/CivilStatusStep.razor): conditional recognition dropdown when married abroad; warning alert for pending recognition\n\n**Tests:** foreign marriage pending → suspend suggested, approve blocked; recognised → normal flow\n\n---\n\n## UI summary (where branches plug in)\n\n| Location | Exception UI |\n|----------|-------------|\n| New birth step | Missing cert warning → suspend CTA |\n| Identity section | Duplicate investigation actions |\n| Residence step | EU/non-EU doc messages; refugee category + federal decision |\n| Civil status step | Marriage recognition pending |\n| Case decision sidebar | Extended checklist (6 questions); illegal stay referral alert; disabled approve with reasons |\n\nFollow existing patterns: `AppAlert`, `AppInfoBox`, MudDialog for suspend/reject, handler injection in page (not child lifecycle loads per [blazor-ef-core rule](.cursor/rules/blazor-ef-core.mdc)).\n\n---\n\n## Tests \u0026 documentation\n\nEach slice: **domain unit tests** + **integration test** (handler pipeline, following [`CaseDecisionTests.cs`](tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/CaseDecisionTests.cs)).\n\nRun: `dotnet test --configuration Release --filter \"Category!=PostgreSQL\"`\n\nDeliverables doc: [`docs/phases/phase-9-exception-scenarios.md`](docs/phases/phase-9-exception-scenarios.md) — slice checklist, demo walkthroughs (one per exception), update [`docs/ROADMAP.md`](docs/ROADMAP.md) Phase 9 status.\n\n---\n\n## Demo walkthroughs (acceptance)\n\n1. **Missing birth cert:** record birth place, no cert → checklist red → suspend → attach cert → resume → approve\n2. **Duplicate:** seed NR match → investigation open → link OR confirm distinct → approve enabled\n3. **EU deepen:** EU citizen without passport → legal residence fails → attach passport → passes\n4. **Refugee:** select refugee, no federal decision → suspend → record decision → waiting register suggested\n5. **Illegal stay:** non-EU, expired/missing permit → referral alert → reject\n6. **Marriage abroad:** married in Morocco, pending recognition → single for register → suspend → mark recognised → proceed\n\n---\n\n## Explicitly out of scope (deferred)\n\n- Diplomat category + `SpecialRegister` rules\n- Homeless reference address + `Shelter`-specific police flow\n- New persisted entities (`InvestigationCase`, `ReferralRecord`) — notes + audit + enum flags suffice for educational demo\n- Birth registration number, parents fields (optional stretch if time permits within 9.1)\n",
  "todos": [
````

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
IsReadyForApproval cannot access Person, so adding marriage recognition blocking there is problematic.

A checklist flag updated by the exception evaluator could handle the blocking without changing IsReadyForApproval. Alternatively, a CanApprove method on RegistrationCase could accept the Person directly.

The cleanest path is an `EnsureReadyForApproval(Person? person)` method on `RegistrationCase`. This lets the domain layer enforce the check instead of relying on the handler.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Adding the DuplicateInvestigationResolved audit action.

GetRegistrationCaseHandler is read-only, so it cannot mutate the case during GET requests. Derived flags like illegalStayDetected and marriage recognition blocking must be computed without persisting changes. A read-only evaluation approach is needed instead of calling the exception evaluator directly.

Birth and duplicate flags will come from persisted checklist values. Illegal stay and marriage recognition will be computed in Map() without applying evaluator results.

Duplicate investigation refreshes will be handled in RecordIdentity and LinkExistingPerson rather than GetRegistrationCaseHandler. This avoids evaluator side effects during read operations.

A read-only `RegistrationExceptionStateCalculator` will compute derived flags without mutating the aggregate. The `RegistrationExceptionEvaluator` will remain for write handlers. This keeps GET requests free of side effects.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Many tests fail because EU citizens now require a passport for legal residence.

Tests expecting EU legal residence without a passport must be updated. Non-EU tests may also need identity documents. A duplicate investigation for Jean Dupont might fail due to person linkage or scoring issues.

The duplicate investigation for Jean Dupont returns None despite an exact name match. The evaluator may not be finding records, or the investigation rule may be failing to open the case. I am checking whether the national registry seeder ran and reviewing the rule logic.

The duplicate investigation rule should set the status to Open when a match scores 100 or higher. The investigation may remain unresolved because the duplicate status was never saved during the identity registration handler.

RecordIdentity persists after the evaluator runs, so the duplicate status should be saved. The investigation may reset to None afterward. The integration test reads the persisted case via GetRegistrationCaseHandler, which should reflect the Open status.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A mismatch exists between the database default and the in-memory Empty() value for duplicate_investigation_resolved. The database field defaults to false, but Empty() sets it to true for new cases.

EF persists DuplicateInvestigationResolved=true from Checklist.Empty() during case creation. The investigation status may be returning None due to an enum conversion failure. The database now stores an empty string default instead of None, which likely breaks enum parsing on read.

The migration needs to default duplicate_investigation_status to "None". duplicate_investigation_resolved should remain true by default; otherwise Empty() fails because EF may skip writing owned entity values on insert. The duplicate test should reach Open status after RecordIdentity unless the evaluator finds no matches. Need to verify whether NationalRegisterRepository.SearchAsync executes in integration tests.

The exception evaluator order for ApplyDuplicateInvestigationRule is being reviewed. When status is None and matches exist, the rule sets status to Open, clears the resolved flag, and returns. The remaining logic then defaults status to None. This sequence may prevent the Open state from persisting correctly.

The evaluator falls through to mark the investigation resolved and set status to None whenever RequiresDuplicateInvestigation is false. This occurs if the person is already linked or if no match reaches the required score threshold. For Jean Dupont, the evaluator likely sees no matches because the national register search returns empty results. The test database may lack seeded data, even though the national register tests normally populate it.

RecordIdentity runs the evaluator after the person is saved, so the person record exists with a PersonId. The duplicate scorer returns a perfect 100% match for Jean Dupont. Test pollution is unlikely in a fresh factory. The GET handler does not re-run the evaluator, so the investigation status is not recalculated on read.

EF should persist the DuplicateInvestigationStatus change because it is a regular property on RegistrationCase. The enum default is zero, which serializes to "None". After RecordIdentity sets the status to Open, EF stores "Open" in the database.
</details></think>

The duplicate test now passes. Four failures remain.

Four remaining failures are being traced to specific test cases. Passport attachment may be failing in RecordResidencePermit_NonEuWorker and PostResidencePermit_NonEuWorker. EuCitizen_WithoutPassport might incorrectly evaluate before as true. FullHappyPath shows a sequence issue where legal residence remains false after satisfy phase9 requirements add passport and birth evidence.

The root cause is identified. `IsReadyForApproval` checks the persisted `BirthEvidenceEstablished` flag on the checklist rather than the computed state. This causes the approval check to fail even after satisfy phase9 attaches the required evidence.

SatisfyPhase9 attaches birth evidence first, then passport, then birth certificate. ApplyBirthEvidenceRule should set the flag true after each attachment. Legal residence should validate once the passport is present. The checklist handler likely rejects the case because it does not account for marriage or illegal stay exceptions. A verbose run of FullHappyPath should reveal whether Approve fails. Duplicate investigation resolution may also be misfiring for unrelated names.

The checklist handler may be using an outdated `IsReadyForApproval` check that ignores exception calculator flags. `AttachDocument` during SatisfyPhase9 might also be failing silently. The domain rule requires both `BirthEvidenceEstablished` and `DuplicateInvestigationResolved` to be true after satisfy phase9.
</details></think>

The new public overload will accept a RegistrationCaseChecklist and three boolean flags: birth evidence established, marriage recognition blocking, and illegal stay detected.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A timing issue may exist when RecordResidencePermitHandler calls EvaluateAndApplyAsync.

The permit is added to the repository but not saved before evaluation. GetByCaseIdAsync then returns null because the database has not yet seen the new record. Fix options are being considered.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
AttachDocumentHandler adds documents through documentRepository.AddAsync.

Documents and permits added but not saved before evaluation cause the same visibility issue. The cleanest fix is extending RegistrationExceptionEvaluator to accept optional overrides for person, permit, and document types.

The fix should be applied at the RegistrationResidenceEvaluator level too. Calling SaveChanges before evaluation was rejected due to side effects. The evaluator methods will accept optional document type overrides.

RecordResidencePermitHandler will pass the permit through RegistrationExceptionEvaluator to the residence evaluator. AttachDocumentHandler will pass the new document type as a pending override instead of relying on unsaved repository state.

RemoveDocument may need to exclude removed documents from the evaluation list. SetResidenceCategoryHandler should be safe since prior steps already save documents. The EuCitizen test flow confirms the bug: attaching a passport without saving it causes evaluation to run against unsaved data and return false.

NonEuWorker is different. Passport is saved during the attach step, so the evaluation sees it. The permit is still missing because it is not saved before evaluation. This is a separate bug from the unsaved document issue.

RemoveDocumentHandler will exclude removed documents from the evaluation list.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Adding optional parameters to pass pending entities.

RemoveDocument may still return removed documents until saved. ExcludedDocumentTypes is needed too. Considering a RegistrationEvaluationContext record with optional overrides instead of many parameters.

Excluding by document type fails when multiple instances exist. Passing the full computed document type list directly is more reliable. AttachDocument will append the new type to existing ones.

Filtering by document type alone would incorrectly remove all instances of that type. The handler must exclude only the specific removed document while preserving duplicates.

RemoveDocumentHandler will explicitly fetch all documents by case ID and filter out the removed document by ID. This avoids relying on Entity Framework tracking behavior before the save operation.

Passing the computed document type list directly to the exception evaluator. Adding permitOverride and document ID override parameters to RegistrationResidenceEvaluator.

RegistrationResidenceEvaluator now accepts optional permitOverride and documentTypesOverride parameters. It falls back to repository lookups when these overrides are absent. RegistrationExceptionEvaluator is being updated with matching optional Person parameters.

AttachDocumentHandler needs access to the residence evaluator to compute document types for the override parameter.
</details></think>

Phase 9 is complete. All todos are done and **150 tests pass** (`Category!=PostgreSQL`).

## Final fix (this session)

Evaluation ran before `SaveChanges`, so newly attached documents and permits were invisible to policy checks. Handlers now pass **pending overrides**:

- `AttachDocumentHandler` — includes the new document type in `documentTypesOverride`
- `RemoveDocumentHandler` — excludes the removed document
- `RecordResidencePermitHandler` — passes `permitOverride`

Also fixed the `GetCaseReviewChecklistHandler` compile error by adding a `BuildQuestions(RegistrationCaseChecklist, …)` overload on `CaseReviewChecklistMapper`.

## What was delivered

| Slice | Highlights |
|-------|------------|
| **Foundation** | Extended checklist, `DuplicateInvestigationStatus`, `RegistrationExceptionEvaluator`, EF migration |
| **9.1 Birth cert** | `BirthInformation` on person, `RecordBirthInformation` slice + UI, suspend guidance |
| **9.2 EU/non-EU** | Identity document checks in policies; re-eval on attach/remove |
| **9.3 Duplicate** | Open/resolve workflow, blocks approve, `ResolveDuplicateInvestigation` slice |
| **9.4 Refugee** | `RefugeePolicy`, `WaitingRegister`, federal decision suspend path |
| **9.5 Illegal stay** | Derived flag, referral alert, reject pre-fill |
| **9.6 Marriage** | `MarriageRecognitionStatus`, foreign marriage blocking |

## Documentation

- [docs/phases/phase-9-exception-scenarios.md](docs/phases/phase-9-exception-scenarios.md) — architecture, deliverables, demo walkthroughs
- [docs/ROADMAP.md](docs/ROADMAP.md) — Phase 9 marked complete (diplomat & homeless deferred)
- [docs/phases/README.md](docs/phases/README.md) — index updated
